title: Collaboration session manager with backwards compatibility
purpose: Manages collaboration sessions by creating, listing, and updating session states while supporting both new and legacy directory structures for backwards compatibility.
module_context: Exports types for collaboration templates, metadata, and state. Includes helper functions for name generation, directory checking, and path resolution with support for legacy session structures.

steps.content: Select a random element from the provided array.
Choose two random adjectives and one random noun.
Combine them in adjective-adjective-noun format to form a session name.
Attempt to retrieve file system statistics for the given path.
IF the path exists and is a directory, return true.
ELSE return false.
Construct and return the path to the .collab directory within the base directory.
Construct and return the path to the .collab/sessions directory within the base directory.
Check if the .collab directory exists in the base directory.
IF the directory does not exist, return false.
ELSE read the contents of the .collab directory.
For each entry, check if it is a directory and not named 'sessions'.
IF a directory has a collab-state.json file, return true.
ELSE return false.
Initialize an empty array to collect session information.
Define a helper function to read sessions from a given directory.
IF the directory does not exist, return the empty session list.
ELSE read the contents of the directory.
For each entry, check if it is a directory and not named 'sessions'.
IF a valid session directory is found, attempt to read its collab-state.json file.
IF the collab-state.json file exists, parse its contents into a state object.
Try to read the metadata.json file for template information.
IF the metadata.json file exists, parse its contents into a metadata object.
IF a session with the same name already exists in the list, skip it.
ELSE add the session information to the list.
IF reading the directory fails, continue to the next entry.
Read sessions from the new location (.collab/sessions/).
Read sessions from the old location (.collab/) for backwards compatibility.
Sort the collected sessions by last activity in descending order.
Return the sorted list of sessions.

methods.name: createCollabSession
methods.normalized_params: (baseDir: string, template: CollabTemplate, customName?: string): Promise<{ name: string; path: string }>

steps.content: Determine the path to the sessions directory.
IF the sessions directory does not exist, create it recursively.
Generate a session name if one is not provided.
Ensure the generated name is unique by checking both new and old locations.
IF a unique name cannot be generated after 10 attempts, throw an error.
Create the session directory structure including diagrams and documents subdirectories.
Generate the current timestamp for creation and activity tracking.
Create and write metadata.json with session details.
Create and write collab-state.json with initial state information.
Return the session name and path.
Check if the session exists in the new location (.collab/sessions/).
IF the session exists in the new location, return its path.
ELSE check if the session exists in the old location (.collab/<sessionName>).
IF the session exists in the old location, return its path.
ELSE return null.
Find the path to the specified session.
IF the session path is not found, throw an error.
Read the collab-state.json file from the session path.
IF the file cannot be read, throw an error.
Parse the contents of the file into a state object.
Return the parsed state object.

methods.name: updateCollabSessionState
methods.normalized_params: (baseDir: string, sessionName: string, updates: Partial<CollabState>): Promise<CollabState>

steps.content: Find the path to the specified session.
IF the session path is not found, throw an error.
Read the current state from the session's collab-state.json file.
IF the file cannot be read, throw an error.
Parse the contents of the file into a current state object.
Merge the provided updates with the current state.
Update the last activity timestamp in the new state.
Write the updated state back to the collab-state.json file.
Return the updated state object.
Find the path to the specified session.
IF the session path is found, return it.
ELSE return the path to the new location for the session.
Construct and return the path to the session in the new location.
